name: Washita Valley Bank
description_short: Washita Valley Bank is a 5-star rated FDIC-insured community bank operating since 1902, offering full-service banking including checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, loans, and online banking.
description_long:
Washita Valley Bank has served as a hometown financial institution since 1902, establishing itself as a community-focused bank in Oklahoma. Under the leadership of CEO/President Michael Sebastian, the bank emphasizes customer service and personal relationships, positioning itself as a modern alternative to impersonal larger institutions while maintaining traditional banking values. The bank achieved a 5-star rating and was ranked among the Top 100 small banks in Oklahoma as of 2015.

Washita Valley Bank offers comprehensive personal banking products including checking and savings accounts, Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with maturities ranging from 6 to 24 months (minimum $2,500 deposit), and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) with a $100 minimum deposit. Additional services include personal loans, bill pay functionality, online and mobile banking platforms, debit/ATM cards, and surcharge-free ATM access through the TransFund ATM Locator network. The bank uses the daily balance method to calculate interest on all interest-bearing accounts and compounds interest semi-annually on most products.

The bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on personalized customer service, long operational history, and community banking approach. Updated banking hours (effective August 22, 2025) reflect expanded Friday hours (9:00 AM - 5:00 PM for lobby and drive-thru) and reduced weekday hours (9:00 AM - 3:00 PM Monday-Thursday), suggesting operational adjustments to serve their customer base. Interest rates are variable and subject to change at the bank's discretion, with penalty structures clearly outlined for early CD and IRA withdrawals.

As a traditional community bank, Washita Valley Bank is best suited for customers seeking personalized service and relationship-based banking rather than digital-first or discount banking experiences. Prospective customers should note that the bank does not appear to offer promotional rates or competitive advantages typical of larger institutions, and current APY information requires contacting the bank directly or visiting their rate sheet.
services: Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) with 12-month auto-renewable certificates
Surcharge-free ATM access via TransFund network
pros: FDIC-insured bank with 5-star rating and 120+ years of operational history since 1902
Low minimum deposits for IRAs ($100) and accessible CD products ($2,500 minimum)
Transparent penalty structure for early CD/IRA withdrawals (1 month's interest for ≤1 year terms, 3 months for >1 year)
Surcharge-free ATM access through TransFund network with mobile ATM locator app
Full-service banking including checking, savings, loans, bill pay, and online/mobile banking
Community-focused institution with emphasis on personalized customer service and local presence
Semi-annual interest compounding on CDs with optional early interest withdrawal capability
cons: Significantly reduced weekday banking hours (9:00 AM - 3:00 PM Monday-Thursday) limit accessibility for traditional work-schedule customers
Variable interest rates subject to change at bank's discretion with no rate guarantees or promotional offerings mentioned
No current rate information displayed on website; customers must contact bank directly or access separate rate sheet to compare APYs
Limited online information about loan products, terms, and rates for personal borrowing
Small bank may lack advanced digital banking features or investment products available at larger institutions

contact.address: 204 N Hazlett St, Fort Cobb, OK 73038

best_for: Local Oklahoma residents seeking relationship-based community banking with personalized service
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance safety and long-term institutional stability over competitive rates
Savers interested in CDs and IRAs with transparent, predictable early withdrawal penalties
Individuals with flexible schedules who can visit during expanded Friday hours (9:00 AM - 5:00 PM)
diagnosis: Washita Valley Bank is best for customers in Oklahoma seeking traditional, personalized community banking with FDIC safety and a 120-year institutional track record. The primary caveat is that reduced weekday hours (9 AM-3 PM) and variable rates without published APYs require direct contact with the bank, making rate shopping and account access more cumbersome than digital-first competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
